title: coronilla emerus subsp. emeroides
intro: Coronilla emerus subsp. emeroides, also known as the dwarf coronilla, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Coronilla emerus subsp. emeroides include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation, making it a popular natural remedy in traditional medicine. Coronilla emerus subsp. emeroides can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
